summary: free-anthropic-claude ships a postinstall dropper that fetches and executes a Python crypto-wallet stealer
details: Package impersonates the Anthropic Claude SDK (package.json name `free-anthropic-claude`, description 'Official Anthropic Claude SDK wrapper'), but the shipped README is for an unrelated 'cachesync-helper' project and the code is a credential-theft dropper. `package.json` declares `postinstall: node lib/cli.js`, which constructs a cache helper whose constructor schedules a `_prefetch` routine after a 3–7s random delay. `_prefetch` HTTPS-POSTs (with `rejectUnauthorized: false`) to four hardcoded bare IPs on port 8443 — 104.194.134.33, 104.194.133.89, 107.189.20.82, 107.189.20.146 — receives a JSON map of base64-encoded files, writes them to disguised persistence directories (`~/Library/Application Support/com.apple.security` on macOS, `~/.local/share/prometheus` on Linux, `~/AppData/Local/Microsoft/Windows Security/Health` on Windows), and spawns `python3 main.py` detached so the process survives the install. A `.cs_v2` marker file prevents re-deploy. If no usable Python is present, the dropper auto-installs one — downloading `Miniconda3-latest-Linux-<arch>.sh` from repo.anaconda.com and executing it on Linux, running `winget install Python.Python.3.12` on Windows, or `brew install python3` on macOS — then pip-installs cryptocurrency-wallet libraries (`bip-utils`, `mnemonic`, `eth-account`) before launching the payload. Sensitive identifiers throughout `lib/index.js` are built by `Array#join` to evade static analysis (`module['constr'+'uctor']['_l'+'oad']`, `['child','_pro','cess'].join('')`, `['exec','File','Sync'].join('')`, `['ba','se','64'].join('')`, `['py','thon','3'].join('')`). The combination of typosquat impersonation, postinstall auto-execution, hardcoded bare-IP C2 with TLS verification disabled, alternate-runtime bootstrap, OS-security-themed persistence directories, and crypto-wallet pip dependencies is an unambiguous wallet-stealer dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
